reforms.description: The Finnish Multimedia Programme, a three-year research venture, focuses on developing multimedia services through collaboration between TEKES and major telecommunications companies, enhancing technology transfer and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The programme fosters innovation in multimedia services, which can enhance productivity and create new market opportunities.
reforms.status_evidence: The Finnish Multimedia Programme was presented as a new initiative to develop multimedia services.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant effort to enhance Finland's technological capabilities and support the growth of a new industry sector.
reforms.source_quote: The Finnish Multimedia Programme, a three-year research venture on the development of multimedia services, is a joint venture of TEKES and the main telecommunications concerns.

reforms.description: Finland has increased its participation in EU research programmes, EUREKA, CERN, and the European Space Agency, enhancing its research capabilities and international collaboration.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased participation in international research initiatives can lead to enhanced knowledge transfer and innovation, boosting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Finland has increased its participation in EU research programmes and other international collaborations.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant commitment to enhancing Finland's research landscape and fostering international collaboration.
reforms.source_quote: Finland has increased its participation in EU research programmes, EUREKA, CERN and the European Space Agency.

reforms.description: Measures to encourage entrepreneurship include changing acts on income tax and the taxation of business income to allow firms that are not limited companies to reinvest a larger share of their profits.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the capital structure of SMEs, the reform enhances their ability to reinvest and grow, which can lead to job creation and economic dynamism.
reforms.status_evidence: Measures to encourage entrepreneurship are being adopted.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant change in the taxation framework for SMEs, which is crucial for fostering entrepreneurship and economic recovery.
reforms.source_quote: Measures to encourage entrepreneurship are being adopted. The capital structure of SMEs is being improved by changing the acts on income tax and the taxation of business income.

reforms.description: The government plans to increase the number of vocational training places substantially, targeting 115,000 in 1996 to address unemployment and skill mismatches.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing vocational training places enhances the skill set of the workforce, improving employability and addressing skill shortages in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The number of training places is now expected to reach 115,000 in 1996.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly expands the vocational training system, which is essential for adapting the workforce to changing economic conditions.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to increase the number of vocational-training places substantially and by more than foreseen in the Employment Programme.

reforms.description: The Finnish government announced reforms to enhance vocational skills and entrepreneurship by integrating entrepreneurship themes into the curriculum of comprehensive schools and secondary vocational institutions.
reforms.package_name: Development Plan for Education and University Research
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By promoting entrepreneurship education, the reform aims to enhance the skills necessary for self-employment and innovation, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms were announced as part of the government's Employment Programme.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant shift in educational focus towards entrepreneurship, which is crucial for adapting to changing economic needs and enhancing workforce skills.
reforms.source_quote: In order to enhance skills and competences related to entrepreneurship and self employment, comprehensive schools and secondary vocational institutions should include an entrepreneurship 'theme' in their curriculum.

reforms.description: The 1995 Development Plan for Education and University Research includes measures to streamline vocational schools and develop a system of higher vocational education, aiming to improve the responsiveness of education to labor market needs.
reforms.package_name: 1995 Development Plan for Education and University Research
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By aligning education with labor market demands, the reform aims to enhance the skills of the workforce, thereby increasing productivity and employment opportunities.
reforms.status_evidence: Measures were announced in the context of the 1995 Development Plan.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform addresses significant deficiencies in vocational education, aiming to improve alignment with labor market needs, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: A number of measures announced in the context of the 1995 Development Plan for Education and University Research are intended to resolve such weaknesses.

reforms.description: The Government sets up a venture capital investment company (the Finnish Industrial Investment Company) to finance primary capital investments in small and medium-sized enterprises.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing capital to small and medium-sized enterprises, the initiative aims to stimulate innovation and economic growth through enhanced business development.
reforms.status_evidence: The Government has established the venture capital investment company.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a venture capital investment company represents a significant structural change aimed at enhancing the financial support available to innovative small and medium-sized enterprises, which is crucial for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The Government sets up a venture capital investment company (the Finnish Industrial Investment Company) to finance primary capital investments in small and medium-sized enterprises.

reforms.description: The Finnish government established the Finnish Industrial Investment Company in February 1995 to allocate 'soft' loans to SMEs, funded by privatisation proceeds, aimed at strengthening industrial technology and know-how.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing financial support to SMEs, the reform aims to enhance their technological capabilities, which can lead to increased productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The Finnish Industrial Investment Company was established and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the Finnish Industrial Investment Company represents a significant step in leveraging privatisation proceeds for enhancing SME capabilities, which is crucial for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: In May 1996 the government has decided to use part of the privatisation proceeds to strengthen industrial technology and know-how, in particular in Small and Medium-sized Enterprises (SMEs).

reforms.description: The Finnish government launched an initiative to increase access to venture capital for SMEs, particularly in high-technology sectors, through the establishment of the Finnish Industrial Investment Company (FIIC).
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing access to venture capital, the initiative supports the growth of innovative firms, which can lead to job creation and increased economic activity.
reforms.status_evidence: The FIIC was established and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to bolster the SME sector, which is crucial for employment and innovation in Finland.
reforms.source_quote: To encourage the creation and growth of SMEs, the government launched an initiative to increase their access to venture capital, especially in the booming high-technology area.

reforms.description: The Finnish government recommended increasing research spending as a share of GDP from 2.2% in 1994 to 2.7% by the year 2000 to enhance long-term job creation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing research funding is expected to stimulate innovation and productivity, leading to job creation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendation was made by the Presidential Working Group on Employment.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative aims to significantly enhance public investment in research, which is critical for maintaining Finland's competitive edge in innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The Presidential Working Group on Employment... recommended that research spending as a share of GDP be further increased from 2.2 per cent in 1994 to 2.7 per cent by the year 2000.

reforms.description: The Oulu science park, established in the early 1990s, played a crucial role in the emergence of the telecommunications industry in Finland, particularly with Nokia.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of the science park facilitated collaboration between research institutions and industry, leading to innovation and economic growth in the telecommunications sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The telecommunications industry emerged from an initiative in the Oulu science park.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the Oulu science park represents a significant structural change in Finland's approach to fostering innovation and industry collaboration, with lasting impacts on the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The booming telecommunications industry (Nokia) emerged from an initiative in the Oulu science park in the early 1990s.
